title: quercus lusitanica subsp. alpestris
intro: Quercus lusitanica subsp. alpestris is a subspecies of the holm oak tree (Quercus lusitanica), native to the Iberian Peninsula and known for its adaptability to high-altitude environments. Traditionally, the plant's bark and leaves have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The health benefits of Quercus lusitanica subsp. alpestris include its potential to act as an antiseptic, antibacterial, and antiviral agent, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
